sections.concept: Row seating. North vs South facing.
sections.formula: Left/Right changes based on facing direction.

examples.question: 5 people facing North. A at left end. B right to A.

sections.formula: Center: Left is CW, Right is ACW.

examples.question: 4 people at round table. A opposite B. C left of A.
examples.solution:
Step 1: Draw circle.
Step 2: Place A bottom. B Top.
Step 3: Left of A is right side. C is there.

shortcuts: Start with the person whose position is most defined.
If facing South, Left becomes Right.
commonMistakes: Not creating multiple cases (possibilities) for ambiguous clues.
placementTips: Practice implies specific direction (North) if not stated.
Circular arrangement is very high value (5 questions set).
